violations.text: Areas of the physical facilities (walls/floor) in the warehouse and kitchen areas were observed with gaps or small holes. Physical facilities are to be maintained and to eliminate possible pest entry points to aid in the current pest control program. (Per the PIC, the warehouse area will need more time for filling /closing gaps and possible entry points.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

At the time of inspection, some droppings were observed along walls/behind equipment in the catering kitchen area. Per the PIC, daily cleaning is conducted and logged for pest control awareness. PIC is to continue frequent/daily cleaning to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of droppings under and behind equipment in the kitchen and warehouse areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per the PIC, the current pest control program is scheduled 3x weekly with a mass trapping scheduled for "Winter Break". New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

Areas of the physical facilities in the warehouse and kitchen areas were observed with gaps in the floors (and cracked/sinking tiles), floor/wall junctures, and windowsill(s). Physical facilities are to be maintained and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

At the time of inspection, droppings were observed along walls/behind equipment in the catering kitchen area. Per the PIC, daily cleaning is conducted and logged for pest control awareness. PIC is to continue frequent/daily cleaning to aid in the current pest control program. (Cleaning of areas began at the time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a large accumulation of droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The garage door to the Bay 2 loading dock outside is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of various equipment storage shelving were observed to have an accumulation of rodent droppings and other debris. Equipment was moved for cleaning if surfaces and equipment at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The floor in the warehouse and kitchen areas was observed to have an accumulation of rodent droppings and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

Areas of the physical facilities in the warehouse and kitchen areas were observed with gaps in the floors, floor/wall junctures, and windowsill(s). Physical facilities are to be maintained and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed under shelving and equipment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per the PIC, pest control is on grounds several days throughout each week. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2024

A garage door to the outside, located in the loading dock area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The floor throughout the warehouse and in the catering kitchen area were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Feta cheese and garlic butter made in house were observed with a date marking exceeding 7 days. PIC voluntarily disposed of food items at the time of inspection. Refrigerated, READY-TO-EAT, TIME/TEMPERATURE CONTROL FOR SAFETY FOOD prepared in a FOOD ESTABLISHMENT shall be discarded if it exceeds 7 days. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The floors and shelving throughout the kitchen area has an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
